rulings.Case Title: SANTIAGO ROBLETO VS. JULIA VARGAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further & Proper Responses To Vargas'S First Set Of Req.'S For Admissions; Memo Of P & A
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 21, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT JULIA VARGAS' Re-Noticed Motion To Compel Further & Proper Responses To Vargas' First Set Of Requests For Admissions. The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Renne as follows: Denied as untimely noticed. CCP § 2033.290(c) requires that a notice of motion to compel must be filed within 45 days after receipt of the allegedly improper response. It appears from the papers that Defendant Julia Vargas did file an initial notice of her intent to file a motion to compel within the 45 days specified in the statute; however, when it was pointed out by Plaintiffs that the papers filed with the initial motion did not include the required separate statement, Defendant, rather than proceeding with the properly noticed motion, chose to withdraw the pending motion and filed what it described as a "Re-Notice of Motion" on October 2, 2012. This notice of motion was outside the period provided for by CCP § 2033.290(c) and a party's unilateral action in extending the period of time is ineffective. The court will impose no monetary sanctions as Plaintiffs' responses to the Requests for Admission were clearly improper and, had this motion been timely filed the court would have ordered Plaintiffs to either admit or deny the requests. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: UNIVERSAL INTERACTIVE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al VS. CONTINENTAL CASUALTY COMPANY, AN ILLINOIS et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 21, 2012, Line 8, DEFENDANTS AMERICAN E & S INSURANCE BROKERS CALIFORNIA, INC. and ALLISON CALDWELL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendants shifted the burden by presenting evidence that they never communicated with or misrepresented anything to Plaintiffs and provided the coverage requested. Plaintiffs fail to submit evidence sufficient to raise a triable issue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RAYNE VAN KING VS. STATE FARM LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, AN ILLINOIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Rayne King'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To General Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 21, 2012, Line 10, Plaintiff Rayne King's Motion To Compel Further Responses To General Form Interrogatories. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Watson as follows: Granted. Disclosure of the facts and documents regarding Ms. King's employment, whether it be solely by Mr. Weitzen or also by State Farm, is reasonably necessary to permit the parties to fully litigate the nature and legal status of her employment. The assertion of an attorney-client privilege, without a log or other supporting data, does not support the refusal to disclose information or documents. The court adds that State Farm shall produce all responsive documents and a privilege log for all documents where attorney-client privilege is claimed by December 20, 2012. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EHG)
